prose:
Based on a Fukui reactivity analysis, the sites of acrolein most susceptible to electrophilic and nucleophilic attack have been identified. The calculation was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (the `standard` tier) in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and multiplicity of 1.

The Fukui function `f+` indicates the change in electron density when an electron is added, highlighting sites susceptible to **nucleophilic attack**. The Fukui function `f-` indicates the change when an electron is removed, highlighting sites susceptible to **electrophilic attack**.

- The site most susceptible to nucleophilic attack is the terminal carbon atom **C4**, with the largest `f+` value of **0.64232**.
- The site most susceptible to electrophilic attack is the oxygen atom **O1**, with the largest `f-` value of **0.39389**.

The full Fukui indices for all 8 atoms are as follows:

| Atom | Symbol | f+ (Nucleophilic Attack) | f- (Electrophilic Attack) | f0 (Radical Attack) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | O | 0.40136 | **0.39389** | 0.39763 |
| 2 | C | 0.02226 | 0.00599 | 0.01412 |
| 3 | C | 0.43405 | 0.09140 | 0.26272 |
| 4 | C | **0.64232** | 0.08737 | 0.36485 |
| 5 | H | -0.15726 | 0.08596 | -0.03565 |
| 6 | H | -0.20720 | 0.18568 | -0.01076 |
| 7 | H | -0.06212 | 0.08449 | 0.01118 |
| 8 | H | -0.07341 | 0.06523 | -0.00409 |

The calculation completed successfully with no warnings reported by the engine.
